linkedin_profile.headline: Guest Experience Manager (Pre-opening) at Montcalm Collection
linkedin_profile.about:
I am a Hospitality Management professional with a great passion for the Luxury Hotel Industry. I am highly motivated to learn, take on new challenges and provide excellent customer service "from the heart" by applying highest level of standards such as FORBES, AA, LQA... My professionalism, skills and confidence have been built through a broad range of industry-related working experience in Front Desk, Guest relations, Reservations and F&B.

Total Experience: 9 years and 3 months

experience.title: Administrative Assistant
experience.description: I was familiarised with basic computing skills such a Microsoft Office, fast typing, sorting insurances application and inserting information through SAP system

experience.title: Student Representative
experience.description: This was my first year in England. I overcame cultural differences and language barriers to take on my first leadership role. My task includes: - Representing the students to raise the school Board of Committee awareness, being the link of exchanging information between the students and teachers - Collecting students feedbacks, listening to their enquiries, observing any issues among the students and approaching them to find the best solutions - Monthly meeting with the teachers to share new innovative ideas and feedbacks from the students

experience.title: Guest Relations Officer
experience.description: I was the first Guest Relations Officer in the hotel and was assigned to look after VIP guests in the Executive Lounge. My daily duties were: - Setting up table and breakfast counters - Check on the presentation, cleanliness and food quality - Meeting and greeting guests during breakfast, afternoon tea and bottomless cocktail dinner - Supervising the service and attending to guests' requests, supporting my team during busy operations - Ensuring all the guests were happy with their stay and reporting back to Executive Lounge Manager if there is any issue - Giving out TripAdvisor cards and taking feedbacks on behalf of the hotel

experience.title: Waitress
experience.description: A part-time role while studying full time in University. With previous experience in waitressing and general food handling, I built up on other skills such as customer service, communication, problem-solving, working under pressure and teamwork. I also rotated around different areas: - Front of house: waitress, hostess - Bar: stewarding, bartender, training of different kind of drinks - Kitchen: food preparation, quality check, food serving, stock check

experience.title: Kitchen Restaurant Trainee
experience.description: I gained knowledge in wine tasting, wine pairing, health and safety, menu planning. I spent my time to learn the basics of restaurant operations in both front and back of house, where I practiced waitressing, bartending, hostess and commis chef. - Setting up tables, preparing before lunch service - Serving table of 1 to 30 guests - Making drinks and preparing cocktail of the days - Learning new menu - Preparing, cooking and presenting food in different sections - starters, mains and desserts, under supervision of the head chef. I was able to build on my teamwork and communication skills. I also learn to take responsibility, problem-solving and time-managing skills, all those that are important for my future career.

experience.title: Guest Relations Officer
experience.organization: Shangri-La Hotel, At The Shard, London
experience.description: 1-year placement programme where I worked in a full-time position as a Guest Relations Officer. I was introduced to Hotel Operating Systems such as OPERA, ALICE, Connect, TrustYou... I also perfected Forbes training standards, including arrival, departure, guest handlings and communication. I was able to develop various abilities, technical and interpersonal skills through different duties: - Customer service, listening: exchanging guests email pre, during and post-stay, arranging amenities for different purposes of stay, answering phone calls, in-room check-in and special care for VIP guests (FHR, Diamond/ Jade member, CEOs...). - Decision-making, problem-solving, resilient and empathy: dealing with guests' requests and complaints, reporting back to my Guest Relations Manager. - Attention to details: decorating rooms and events to the highest standards. - Computing, Microsoft office, analytical: analysing daily VIP report, arrival report 7 to 1 days before guests' arrivals. - Teamwork, communication, flexibility: cooperating with different internal departments daily such as Housekeeping, In-room dining, Concierge and Marketing, as well as external such as Travel Agents. My tasks also cover Receptionist with check-in, check-out, room assignment, dealing with payment, billing, guests' complaints, updating profile and credit checks. I also took initiative to learn and cross-trained in various departments such as Finance, Communication, Reservation, Housekeeping, Events and Restaurant. I was willing to cover shifts for many positions such as Restaurant/Bar hostess, In-room dining server, Housekeeping attendance and Concierge. I have gained a lot of experience during this time and achieved monthly rewards for excellent customer services and reviews.

experience.title: Guest Relations Coordinator
experience.organization: Shangri-La Hotel, At The Shard, London
experience.description: After finishing my placement year, I got a new contract with the company for a part-time Guest Relations Officer position. Through commitment and self-management, I can balance between my full-time study at University and working on the weekends, usually during the busiest operation as I can work well under pressure while still maintain excellent service and standards. With my previous experience in the same role, I continue to build on important skills: - Communication, listening, teamwork and professionalism: in charge of coordinating my colleagues, working as a team to deliver pleasant and smooth stays to our guests. - Task delegation, multi-tasking, organizing: distribute daily tasks, handle phone calls/ emails, arrival reports, as well as assist the front desk with check-in/out during rush hours. - Flexibility and adaptability: I cover shifts for Receptionist, In-room dining server and Housekeeping when needed.

education.degree_or_field: Bachelor's Degree with First Class Honours, Hospitality Administration/Management

I am an extremely self-motivated individual, I feel and believe the most rewarding aspect of this industry is developing, empowering and leading people, working towards a greater customer experience. Working in a Hotel can be complex and survival depends on thinking and acting strategically. My main objective is driving the business and my team forward, while planning ahead is imperative I am flexible enough to accommodate innovation; flexible enough to encourage new ideas and overall objectives are not compromised but enhanced. linkedin_profile.headline: Dynamic and people-centred Director of Rooms Operations with over 15 years of experience in luxury hospitality across London’s most iconic properties.
linkedin_profile.about:
With over 25 years experience in the hospitality industry, I am currently working as a GM for the Montcalm Luxury Hotel group in their flagship property located in Marble Arch, London. My many years in the industry have shaped me to be an adaptable individual, unwavering in my dedication to deliver exceptional hospitality and service. I consider myself to be a natural leader, organised and able to prioritize and delegate tasks with ease. I take great pride in developing my team, who I consider as an extension of and representation of myself.

Total Experience: 21 years and 10 months

experience.title: Shift Leader, Front Office
experience.description: • Handling complaints and issues made by both non-residents and residents and finding a solution to ensure guest satisfaction. • Record keeping and handling of incidents and accidents within the workplace. • Completing weekly, daily and monthly reports to a high standard and on time • Assisting reception with their day-to-day duties including checking in and out. Room assignment and checking banking. • Structuring a daily VIP list and ensuring VIP guests receive the appropriate treatment and all their requests are adhered to being their main point of contact throughout their time with us. • Daily meet and greet of all guests especially VIP guests. • Assisting and supervising in every department from food and beverage outlets to housekeeping. • Fire Team co-ordinator and member.

experience.title: Night Manager Duty Manager
experience.description: I was promoted and transferred to the London hotel as a Night Manager and Duty Manager. As a Night Manager my main duties and responsibilities were to ensure the smooth running of the front of house areas of the hotel and acts as the main point of contact for all guest assistance. Represent the Hotel Management in their absence at night and taking full responsibilities of the hotel. Managed, record and promptly resolved any issue that aroused. I would also act in accordance with the company secure and emergency procedures.

experience.title: Front Of House Manager
experience.description: The Washington Mayfair Hotel, London (five star, 171 bedroom) The Courthouse Doubletree by Hilton (five star, 112 bedroom) The Bentley, Waldorf Astoria (5 star hotel, 64 bedroom) As a front office manager I reported to the General Manager, a key part of this role was to create a special welcoming and memorable experience for the guests. I was responsible for the smooth registration and cashiering functions of the front desk. Overseen the team, ensuring accurate communication flow-up on any problems, guests, requests and special requirements. I would also review arrivals, VIP’s, special requests, group needs and worked closely with the housekeeping departments in room assignment and guests needs. In addition I would together with Director of Revenue worked on maximize the ADR by monitoring and managing booking engines, company website as well as third parties.

experience.description:
As the Hotel manager of Montcalm Luxury Hotel Group, I am responsible for the implementation of brand standards across all the hotels, ensuring guest satisfaction is maximised and expectations are always met and exceeded. Guest interaction represents one of my key strengths and allows me to build strong personal relationships, resulting in significant repeat business. I achieve my goals by seeking to deliver a personalised level of customer care that exceeds expectations. In order to facilitate the best levels of service and hospitality across the hotel, I seek to inspire and motivate my team to ensure they share the same beliefs and enthusiasm. I work closely with the management team, setting clear objectives for my team to give them both autonomy and accountability for delivering against these goals. I strongly believe in succession planning, developing and growing the talent pipeline.

experience.title: Head Of Hospitality Prolific Private Client State-of-the-Art Private Residence, London
experience.organization: Prolific Private Client | State-of-the-Art Private Residence, London
experience.description: Led the hospitality operations for an ultra-high-net-worth family, managing a portfolio of London and overseas residences. The role demanded a balance of precision, discretion, and creativity in delivering exceptional guest experiences for A-listers, dignitaries, and VIPs. Some of my Key accomplishments include: – Directed and inspired a front-of-house team to uphold exacting service standards across multiple private properties. – Oversaw the setup and operation of international estates, ensuring seamless coordination and brand continuity. – Conceptualized and executed intimate high-profile events, from private dinners to large-scale celebrations. – Ensured the meticulous preservation and care of fine art, antiques, and luxury furnishings within all residences. – Managed full-cycle recruitment, onboarding, and performance coaching, strengthening staff retention and morale. – Developed bespoke service guidelines and household SOPs, enhancing efficiency and service precision. – Maintained utmost confidentiality and professionalism while managing every detail of the family’s lifestyle operations.

experience.title: Director Of Rooms Operations, St Pancras London, Autograph Collection, 5 , London, UK
experience.organization: St Pancras London, Autograph Collection, 5*, London, UK
experience.description: Leading the full Rooms Division for one of London’s most architecturally iconic hotels, overseeing Front of House, Loss Prevention, Spa, and Housekeeping operations. The role focuses on delivering elevated, story-driven luxury experiences that reflect the hotel’s heritage as part of the Autograph Collection brand. Strategic Highlights & Operational Impact: –– Directed the brand transition from Renaissance to Autograph Collection (June 2025), establishing a new service philosophy aligned with TIME brandmark. – Implemented a redefined guest-journey framework, introducing signature arrival rituals and bespoke recognition moments for Elite Members. – Improved Guest Satisfaction Intent to Recommend from 58.4 to 66.9 within six months through targeted service recovery and coaching. – Enhanced communication flow through Weekly Operations Meetings, fostering proactive issue resolution across Rooms, F&B and Engineering teams. – Recognized by Marriott International EMEA for driving service transformation and brand elevation during property rebranding. – Selected for the Hotel Manager Development Track (Marriott International) in recognition of outstanding leadership and exceptional performance in role.

experience.title: AREA REVENUE MANAGER
experience.description: Initially Revenue Manager for Dorint The Hague and later promoted to Cluster Revenue Manager responsible for the 6 Accor hotels in The Hague. ***** Sofitel (143 rooms) **** Dorint (216 rooms) **** Novotel (106 rooms) **** Mercure (159 rooms) *** Ibis City Centre (197 rooms) *** Ibis Scheveningen (88 rooms) Main responsibilities were: Prepare annual statistics and assists in the actualisation, implementation and realization of budget and marketing plan in conjunction with the General Managers Monitoring and reporting of pick-up, lead times and business trends Rate planning and active revenue/yield management Monitoring of GDS and CRS systems and setting up of media campaigns i.e. TravelClick Ensuring that Electronic Distribution Systems are set up correctly and used effectively to maximise distribution, accommodation yield and revenues Analysing and setting up of rate structure, forecasting and strategic planning including the commercial co-ordination of group and conference requests Maintaining and further developing business as well as relationship management with clients Hosting of weekly revenue Strategy Meetings to communicate strategy and develop corrective action for low dates or periods Coaching and guiding of the various reservations and front office departments and sales and marketing teams to increase revenue management knowledge and promote internal sales as well as up-selling and cross-selling amongst the various properties Preparation of daily, weekly and monthly reports in accordance with company standards and procedures i.e. production reports, business mix, business on the books, forecasts etc.

experience.title: AREA DIRECTOR MIDDLE EAST - ME By MELIA
HEAD OF HOTELS
experience.description: The Montcalm Collection is a group of independent luxury hotels located throughout London. The collection includes four main properties: Montcalm East in Shoreditch, Montcalm Marble Arch in Marylebone, Montcalm Royal London House in Finsbury Square, and Montcalm Brewery in Barbican. Each hotel within the Montcalm Collection has its own unique story and design, blending classic and contemporary elements to reﬂect the character of London. Montcalm Brewery is a historic former brewery transformed into a ﬁve-star hotel, while Montcalm Royal London House is a mid-century marvel with a stunning rooftop terrace. The hotels are known for their thoughtful touches, luxurious comfort, and exceptional personal service. Montcalm East is an Autograph Collection hotel with a mission to support its vibrant neighbourhood of East London. In addition to the Montcalm hotels, the collection also includes the: Montcalm Townhouses, Inhabit Hotels (member of Design Hotels) and the Park Grand Hotels. Inhabit Hotels are urban wellness properties in West London. Wellbeing is at the heart of what we do, as we offer guests both restorative and invigorating experiences to foster mindfulness, healthy habits, and even better sleep. We are committed to enhancing green initiatives by monitoring and reducing overall consumption levels and converting environmental efforts into sustainable business opportunities. Social benchmarks are likewise core to our strategic decision-making. Inhabit Hotels have been awarded B Corp™ Certification, reﬂecting our commitment to social and environmental sustainability. Park Grand Hotels are contemporary boutique hotels located in prime locations throughout London. Montcalm Collection provides a range of luxury accommodations in London. Website http://montcalmcollection.com
